tests.expected: pr-report

tests.prompt: Audit this skill for standards issues
Create a new skill from scratch
Run the test suite
Fix the login bug
Write a PRD for the new feature
Merge latest upstream into this branch
Verify the current branch
Debrief ticket PROJ-123
Triage this incoming bug
Plan the next steps for this project

Given a GitHub PR and a configured GitHub MCP server, run /pr-report. The gh CLI is also available. Expect the report to include full top-level review bodies from the GitHub MCP tool, not reconstructed summaries from the CLI.
tests.expected: Top-level review bodies are fetched from the GitHub MCP tool when it is the best available source.

tests.prompt: Given a GitHub PR and no configured MCP server, run /pr-report. Expect the conductor to use the gh CLI or direct GitHub API as the best available tool.
tests.expected: The gh CLI or direct GitHub API is used when no MCP server is available.

tests.prompt: Given no configured PR tool and no MCP server, run /pr-report. Expect the conductor to fall back to manual user input and still produce a report.
tests.expected: Manual user input is used as a fallback and a report is still produced.

tests.prompt: Given a SonarCloud API failure and a configured SonarQube MCP server, run /pr-report. Expect the agent to offer to use the SonarQube MCP server before recording static analysis as unavailable.
tests.expected: The SonarQube MCP server is offered as a fallback before the capability is marked unavailable.

tests.prompt: Given a partial result from the configured PR tool and a better available tool (e.g., GitHub MCP), run /pr-report. Expect the agent to disclose the better tool and ask whether to retry before accepting the partial result.
tests.expected: The better tool is disclosed and the user is asked before degraded data is accepted.

tests.prompt: Given two available tools that return conflicting data for a blocking CI item, run /pr-report. Expect the conductor to record the conflict and ask the user which source to trust.
tests.expected: The conflict is recorded in the Data sources section and the user is consulted for a blocking item.

tests.prompt: Given that only a degraded source is available for CI/build status and pr-report.tooling.degraded_mode is ask, run /pr-report. Expect the report and chat summary to name the degraded tool, the better tool, the impact, and how to override.
tests.expected: Degradation is disclosed with degraded tool, better tool, impact, and override instruction.

tests.prompt: Given pr-report.tooling.degraded_mode is accept and a degraded source is used for static analysis, run /pr-report. Expect the report to still record the degraded source and the better alternative in the Data sources section.
tests.expected: The degraded source and better alternative are recorded even when accepted silently.

tests.prompt: Given no available tool for CI/build status, run /pr-report. Expect the conductor to note the gap and continue without blocking on the missing capability.
tests.expected: The missing capability is noted in the Data sources section and the report continues.

tests.prompt: Given a configured GitHub MCP server that fails to connect, run /pr-report. Expect the conductor to stop and consult the user about the connection failure.
tests.expected: The conductor stops and asks the user about the connection failure.

tests.prompt: Given context compaction mid-run, run /pr-report. Expect the conductor to resume from the pending phase using the state file.
tests.expected: The conductor resumes from the first pending phase after reading the state file.

tests.prompt: Given a corrupted state file for the current PR, run /pr-report. Expect the conductor to archive the state and start fresh.
tests.expected: The corrupted state is archived and the conductor starts fresh.

tests.prompt: Given pr-report.tooling.preference is cli and both an MCP server and gh CLI are available, run /pr-report. Expect the CLI to be preferred over the MCP server for PR metadata.
tests.expected: The user-configured tooling preference overrides automatic ranking.

tests.prompt: Given /pr-report ci, expect the conductor to collect only CI/build status and produce a focused report with the Data sources section and a short summary.
tests.expected: Only the CI/build capability is collected and reported.

tests.prompt: Given pr-report.test_mode is true and local fixtures are present, run /pr-report. Expect the report to be generated from fixtures without live tokens.
tests.expected: The report is generated from local fixtures without calling live tools.
